entityExtensionTemplates.name: VRE PARFE Site Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Describes the renewable energy site configuration — monitoring type, jurisdiction, and Grid Emission Factor baseline. Set once at site commissioning by the issuing authority.

entityExtensionTemplates.name: REC Module Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Configuration for the Renewable Energy Certificate module. Specifies the generation type and the kWh threshold at which a REC token is minted.

entityExtensionTemplates.name: H2O Module Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Configuration for the Water Stewardship Certificate module. Specifies the water source type and the litre threshold at which an H2O token is minted.

entityExtensionTemplates.name: CO2 Module Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Configuration for the Carbon Offset Certificate module. Specifies the source of the Grid Emission Factor (GEF) — either a constant from SCHEMAS_V2 (testnet) or a per-licensee value from the LICENSE_GRANT HCS record (production).

entityExtensionTemplates.name: Variable Template Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Flags that control which variable templates apply for a given monitoring type and whether the variable is required for the physics validation gate.

entityExtensionTemplates.name: Physics Gate Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Records the physics validation configuration for the site. The edge notary rejects any TELEMETRY reading where kwh > maxKwhPerReading. This is a mandatory fraud-prevention gate. capacityMW is stored in the GATEWAY_DEPLOY HCS record at gateway commissioning.

entityExtensionTemplates.name: Ledger Attestation Options
entityExtensionTemplates.description: Records the ledger anchoring configuration. Every TELEMETRY record is written to the Hedera Consensus Service with an Ed25519 cryptographic signature over a SHA-256 hash of the stable-stringified payload. The full Ed25519 public key PEM is embedded in sig.pub of every TELEMETRY message, enabling fully self-contained verification without an external key registry lookup. The schema used to produce the payload is frozen and cannot be mutated post-signing.
